description: Held-out benchmark: clinically established mast-cell stabilizers, MCAS-relevant drugs, and well-supported allergic-disease compounds that are NOT in data/compounds/seeds.json AND NOT in the reference ligand sets in scripts/score_against_targets.py. The benchmark asks: when the full pipeline scores these blind, do they land in the top of the appropriate category ranking?
purpose: Credibility check on the composite ranking. A pipeline that can't recover known actives in their natural category is broken — regardless of how good the headline result looks.

expected_category_definitions.rescue: Approved or commonly-used acute mast-cell stabilizers / H1 / H2 antagonists not in our reference set.
expected_category_definitions.maintenance: Daily-use stabilizers, immunomodulators, anti-inflammatory naturals, and downstream-FcεRI modulators (JAK / BTK / calcineurin) not in our reference set.
expected_category_definitions.remission: Upstream disease-modifying agents — covalent Nrf2/KEAP1 activators, KIT-axis kinase inhibitors. Downstream FcεRI modulators (JAK / BTK) are classified as maintenance instead, per EXP-015 §7.1.

evaluation_metric: recovery@N: fraction of known actives that rank in the top N of their expected category's ranked CSV.

actives.name: Azelastine
actives.evidence: H1 antagonist + documented mast cell stabilizer; approved as intranasal/ophthalmic anti-allergic.

actives.name: Olopatadine
actives.evidence: Dual H1 antagonist + mast cell stabilizer; topical ophthalmic/nasal anti-allergic.

actives.name: Nedocromil
actives.evidence: Cromone class; mast cell stabilizer (asthma + allergic conjunctivitis).

actives.name: Pemirolast
actives.evidence: Mast cell stabilizer; ophthalmic anti-allergic.

actives.name: Tranilast
actives.evidence: Anti-allergic + anti-fibrotic mast cell stabilizer; used in atopic dermatitis + keloid scars; cited in MCAS literature.

actives.name: Lodoxamide
actives.evidence: Cromone class; ophthalmic mast cell stabilizer for vernal keratoconjunctivitis.

actives.name: Amlexanox
actives.evidence: Cromone-like anti-allergic; topical for aphthous ulcers + asthma adjunct; IKK-epsilon inhibitor.

actives.name: Levocetirizine
actives.evidence: Active R-enantiomer of cetirizine; H1 antagonist.

actives.name: Bilastine
actives.evidence: Newer-generation H1 antagonist; minimal CNS penetration.

actives.name: Ebastine
actives.evidence: Long-acting H1 antagonist.

actives.name: Mizolastine
actives.evidence: H1 antagonist + LOX inhibition.

actives.name: Doxepin
actives.evidence: Tricyclic with extremely potent H1 + H2 antagonism; used for refractory urticaria.

actives.name: Cinnarizine
actives.evidence: H1 antagonist + Ca channel blocker; antiallergic + antivertigo.

actives.name: Tofacitinib
actives.category_relabel_note: Relabeled remission → maintenance after EXP-015 audit finding (2026-05-22). JAK1/3 inhibition is downstream of FcεRI signaling, not upstream Nrf2-axis remission. See EXP-015 §7.1.
actives.evidence: JAK1/3 inhibitor; downstream of FcεRI; emerging interest in MCAS-adjacent autoimmunity.

actives.name: Acalabrutinib
actives.category_relabel_note: Relabeled remission → maintenance after EXP-015 audit finding (2026-05-22). BTK inhibition is downstream of FcεRI signaling, not upstream Nrf2-axis remission. See EXP-015 §7.1.
actives.evidence: Selective BTK inhibitor; downstream of FcεRI signaling in mast cells.

actives.name: Tacrolimus
actives.evidence: Calcineurin inhibitor; suppresses mast cell mediator release.

actives.name: Pimecrolimus
actives.evidence: Topical calcineurin inhibitor; suppresses mast cell + T cell activation.

actives.name: Hydroxychloroquine
actives.evidence: Anti-malarial + immunomodulator; reported in some MCAS regimens.

actives.name: Glycyrrhizin
actives.evidence: Licorice triterpene saponin; anti-allergic + anti-inflammatory.

actives.name: Hydroxytyrosol
actives.evidence: Olive polyphenol; Nrf2 activator; anti-inflammatory.

actives.name: Cysteamine
actives.evidence: Aminothiol antioxidant; Nrf2-axis modulator; covalent through thiol-disulfide exchange.
